lastLearnRun: 2026-05-13 lead-pipeline resilience: lead-pipeline-canary-cron, conversion-page-registry-suppresses-competing-ctas, synthetic-probe-source-sentinel-filter, prod-write-script-confirmation-gate, live-canary-as-deploy-validation, parallel-review-agents-with-plan-summary

patterns.name: lead-pipeline-canary-cron
patterns.when: Business-critical workflow depends on multiple external integrations (Supabase, transactional email, CRM, etc.) where silent failure of any one is invisible to the user.
patterns.example: Shared lib/<x>-canary.ts exposing run<X>Canary(): Promise<CanaryResult> with named steps[]. Cron route app/api/cron/<x>-canary/route.ts auth-gated by Authorization: Bearer ${CRON_SECRET} (constant-time-ish compare matching mirror-meta-leads pattern), returns 503 on any blocking step failure. Manual CLI scripts/smoke-<x>-canary.ts with optional --full and SMOKE_CONFIRM_PROD=yes guard. Synthetic rows tagged source: 'health-canary'; lib/<x>-query.ts::getAll filters them out. Self-prune step deletes rows older than 7d (treated as non-blocking for overall ok/fail). Cadence: detection latency < daily, e.g. '0 */6 * * *' for 6-hourly. Reference: lib/leads-canary.ts, app/api/cron/leads-canary/route.ts, scripts/smoke-lead-canary.ts.

patterns.name: conversion-page-registry-suppresses-competing-ctas
patterns.when: App has dedicated conversion-form pages (contact, trial booking, scholarship, etc.) AND has site-wide floating/sticky CTAs that would compete with the page's own submit button.
patterns.example: lib/conversion-pages.ts: CONVERSION_PAGE_PREFIXES (readonly tuple) + isConversionPage(pathname). Match on prefix so nested routes (/contact/thank-you) inherit. Floating components call isConversionPage(usePathname()) and early-return null BEFORE any state hooks that affect layout (so CSS vars like --lbta-sticky-cta-h stay 0px and don't add orphan padding). Unit tests pin the registry to known prefixes including non-matching false-positive guards (/contacts ≠ /contact). Reference: lib/conversion-pages.ts, components/StickyCTA.tsx.

patterns.name: synthetic-probe-source-sentinel-filter
patterns.when: Canary writes to a shared data store also read by a team-facing UI; team should never see synthetic rows.
patterns.example: Tag synthetic rows with a fixed sentinel value (source: 'health-canary' for Supabase leads). Filter at the query layer (getAllLeads adds .neq('source', HEALTH_CANARY_SOURCE)) so the UI can never show probe rows even if a future caller forgets. Constant lives next to the canary code (lib/leads-canary.ts::HEALTH_CANARY_SOURCE) and is imported by both writer and reader — single source of truth. Reference: lib/leads-canary.ts, lib/leads-query.ts::getAllLeads.

patterns.name: prod-write-script-confirmation-gate
patterns.when: CLI script can mutate production state (email send, real contact create, payment) but is convenient to invoke locally.
patterns.example: looksLikeProduction(url) helper checks URL host. When mutating mode + production target → require SMOKE_CONFIRM_PROD=yes. Refuse with exit code 3 (distinct from 1=fail / 2=env-missing) and a clear message explaining what side effects would occur. Default behavior stays safe. Reference: scripts/smoke-lead-canary.ts.

patterns.name: live-canary-as-deploy-validation
patterns.when: Deploy ships a new endpoint or critical path; need stronger proof than 'GET / returns 200'.
patterns.example: After git push + Vercel auto-deploy, hit the new endpoint with the production CRON_SECRET (pulled via vercel env pull --environment production /tmp/<file>) and inspect the JSON response. The response is the literal output of the new code running in the new deploy — much stronger than health:prod which only checks GET 200s on existing routes. Use this in /compound:deploy when an endpoint is net-new.

patterns.name: parallel-review-agents-with-plan-summary
patterns.when: Review phase of /compound:full or /compound:review with a small-to-medium change set (8–15 files).
patterns.example: Launch 3 parallel reviewer subagents (ce-correctness-reviewer, ce-security-reviewer, ce-maintainability-reviewer) each given: (a) plan path or pasted plan summary, (b) explicit list of files changed, (c) the plan's Acceptance checklist, (d) specific scrutiny questions. Wall clock ~10 min. Findings dedup-friendly because each agent has a distinct lens. Yield: 0 critical / 2 warnings / 4 notes for an 11-file change in our incident.

patterns.name: video-source-paths-must-exist
patterns.when: Homepage or hero uses multiple HTML5 video source elements
patterns.example: List only URLs that exist under public/; if MP4 lives only under legacy-working-assets, omit a broken /videos/foo.mp4 source — browsers do not always fall through cleanly. Verify with ls public/videos and Network tab 200s.

patterns.name: hero-video-poster-lcp
patterns.when: Hero uses autoplay video; LCP must not compete with video bytes
patterns.example: Layer next/image priority + fill of hero-poster.webp (small WebP, <200KB ideal); parent wrapper position:relative + relative h-full w-full for fill. Video preload=none, deferred load() after paint, fade in on onPlaying; omit video poster if same URL as Image to avoid double fetch. prefers-reduced-motion: poster only. Do not add root layout <link rel=preload> for a homepage-only asset (other routes pay the cost). OG/destinations still use laguna-horizon.webp or dedicated art. Poster script: scripts/generate-hero-poster.js when hero changes.

patterns.name: nextjs16-proxy-coach-hub
patterns.when: Next.js 16+ intercepts requests before handlers; Coach Hub or similar protected matchers
patterns.example: Root proxy.ts: export function proxy(request: NextRequest) { ... }; export const config = { matcher: ['/coach-hub', '/coach-hub/:path*'] }; same auth logic as legacy middleware. next build lists ƒ Proxy (Middleware).

patterns.name: analytics-scripts-lazyOnload-lcp
patterns.when: Meta Pixel, GA4, or other non-critical scripts compete with LCP / Best Practices
patterns.example: next/script strategy=lazyOnload for fbevents and gtag/js; dns-prefetch (not always preconnect) for facebook connect host. Tradeoff: first pageview may fire slightly later than afterInteractive — document if marketing needs earlier attribution.

patterns.name: single-source-dropdown-options
patterns.when: Modal or page shows a list of programs/options also used elsewhere
patterns.example: Trial program list: one data file or lib helper; TrialBookingModal and book page both import

patterns.name: prices-in-data-only
patterns.when: Component displays pricing (tiers, sessions, membership)
patterns.example: PricingComparison, LeaguesSection read from /data/*.json or lib/programs-data

patterns.name: modal-focus-restore
patterns.when: Any modal/dialog that traps focus
patterns.example: On open save document.activeElement; on close call previousFocus?.focus() in close handler and effect cleanup

patterns.name: icon-button-aria-label
patterns.when: Button has only an icon, no visible text
patterns.example: aria-label="Send message" or "Close"

patterns.name: webhook-error-500
patterns.when: API route processes webhooks
patterns.example: On catch: NextResponse.json({ success: false, error: '...' }, { status: 500 })

patterns.name: reduced-motion-section
patterns.when: Section uses Framer Motion whileInView or entrance animation
patterns.example: useReducedMotion(); when true render plain element with no motion props (DarkSection)

patterns.name: animated-section-use-reduced-motion
patterns.when: Shared component (e.g. AnimatedSection) uses Framer Motion initial/whileInView
patterns.example:
useReducedMotion(); if (reduceMotion) return
{children}
; else return motion.div with animation props. CSS reduced-motion does not gate JS motion.

patterns.name: server-component-json-ld-client-island
patterns.when: Page needs JSON-LD in initial HTML and interactive sections (nav, hero, StickyCTA)
patterns.example: Server page: getCoachesForSchema(); <script type="application/ld+json" dangerouslySetInnerHTML={...} />; <CoachesPageClient />. Client wrapper contains only interactive parts.

patterns.name: section-copy-from-data
patterns.when: Section displays narrative/paragraphs that exist in a data field
patterns.example:
founder.bio.split(/(?<=.)\s+/).filter(Boolean).map((p, i) =>

{p.trim()}

); or bioParagraphs array in JSON

patterns.name: schema-builder-defensive-env
patterns.when: Building JSON-LD (Person, ItemList) from app data
patterns.example: Guard image (only add when present); description: schemaDescription ?? bio ?? ''; base URL from process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL ?? 'https://...'

patterns.name: stable-list-keys
patterns.when: Mapping over items that have slug or order
patterns.example: key={coach.slug ?? `order-${coach.order}`} so keys are stable and unique

patterns.name: anchor-section-scroll-margin
patterns.when: In-page anchor nav with sticky bar; sections have id for #hash links
patterns.example: Add scroll-mt-28 to section roots (CoachesHero, CoachingTeamSection, CoachesCTA) so scroll-to-section does not hide heading under nav

patterns.name: schedule-by-location-single-source
patterns.when: Building a calendar or schedule view by location (e.g. Moulton, Alta, LBHS) for print/PDF or filters
patterns.example: lib/calendar-schedule.ts: getScheduleByLocationByDay(season) uses only getWinter2026Programs, getSpringProgramsForDisplay, etc. from programs-data; normalizeSlotLocation uses slot.location when present else formatLocation(programLocation). No hardcoded program names or prices; view renders same data as schedules page.

patterns.name: skip-to-main-focusable-target
patterns.when: Skip-to-main link (href="#main-content") is present in layout; target must receive focus and provide main landmark
patterns.example: Target element: <main id="main-content" tabIndex={-1}> so focus moves into main when skip is activated; standalone landings (junior-trial, beginner-program) that bypass ConditionalLayout must add their own main#main-content with tabIndex={-1}

patterns.name: next-image-logos
patterns.when: Displaying logos or partner images (not tracking pixel)
patterns.example: Next/Image with width, height, descriptive alt (ChatWidget logo, PartnershipSection)

patterns.name: client-image-fallback-missing-asset
patterns.when: Section uses image paths that may not exist yet (e.g. Why Choose); section must never break with 404 or uncaught error
patterns.example: Client component (WhyChooseImage): next/image with src + fallbackSrc; onError set state to fallback once (guard with failed flag). Document fallback in media brief; create folder + .gitkeep for future assets.

patterns.name: zod-validate-api-body
patterns.when: API route accepts JSON body
patterns.example: validateRequest(schema, body); return 400 on parse/validation failure

patterns.name: stub-api-friendly-reply
patterns.when: Implementing a placeholder API (e.g. chat) without backend/LLM
patterns.example: POST validates with Zod, rate-limits, returns 200 with friendly reply directing user to contact/phone; 500 on error with same fallback message

patterns.name: parseJsonBody-then-validateRequest
patterns.when: API route accepts JSON body
patterns.example: const parsed = await parseJsonBody(request); if (!parsed.ok) return parsed.response; validateRequest(schema, parsed.data); same for all 9 JSON routes including webhook

patterns.name: modal-timeout-cleanup
patterns.when: Modal uses setTimeout or setInterval (e.g. success message then close)
patterns.example: Store timeout id in ref; useEffect cleanup: return () => { if (ref.current) clearTimeout(ref.current); }; clear ref in close() too

patterns.name: restore-focus-on-auto-close
patterns.when: Dialog can close by user action or by timeout
patterns.example: In timeout callback: previousActiveRef.current?.focus(); then setVisible(false). Same focus restore as manual close

patterns.name: trust-stats-from-site-stats
patterns.when: Component shows players trained, years experience, rating, review count
patterns.example: Import siteStats from @/data/site-stats.json; use trustStats (playersCount, yearsExperience, rating, reviewCount); no hardcoded numbers

patterns.name: webhook-id-validate-then-use
patterns.when: Webhook payload contains an ID used in URLs or request bodies (e.g. contactId)
patterns.example: Normalize: id = typeof raw === 'number' ? raw : parseInt(String(raw), 10); if (!Number.isInteger(id) || id < 1) return 400; use only id in URLs and bodies

patterns.name: webhook-secret-timing-safe
patterns.when: Verifying webhook authenticity with a shared secret
patterns.example: Buffer.from(secret, 'utf8'); same length check; crypto.timingSafeEqual(expected, provided); reject on catch or mismatch

patterns.name: hero-cta-on-dark-solid-bg
patterns.when: Primary CTA button sits on a dark hero or gradient background
patterns.example: Use bg-white text-black (or bg-black text-white) with focus ring; avoid text-only (e.g. text-black) on dark gradient for contrast

patterns.name: in-page-anchor-nav
patterns.when: Long single page with multiple sections (e.g. schedules, pricing) where users need to jump
patterns.example: Sticky nav with anchor links; section id + scroll-mt-* on targets; aria-label; min-h-[48px]; scrollIntoView with behavior gated by prefers-reduced-motion (SchedulesAnchorNav)

patterns.name: scroll-into-view-reduced-motion
patterns.when: Using scrollIntoView with behavior: 'smooth' in JS
patterns.example: prefersReducedMotion = matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches; behavior: prefersReducedMotion ? 'auto' : 'smooth'

patterns.name: pull-quote-section-quote
patterns.when: Any blockquote or section quote that should match Brand Guide
patterns.example: Use <PullQuote> or apply class section-quote (gradient left border); avoid inline border-l-2 so one source drives quote styling

patterns.name: parallax-reduced-motion-gate
patterns.when: Page or component has parallax or scroll-driven motion
patterns.example: matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)'); when true disable parallax or use static fallback (HomeHero, About hero)

patterns.name: decorative-svg-aria-hidden
patterns.when: SVG is purely decorative (arrow, chevron, icon in link/button)
patterns.example: Add aria-hidden="true" to the <svg> so assistive tech skips it

patterns.name: error-required-text-lbta-red
patterns.when: Form error messages or required field indicators (e.g. asterisk)
patterns.example: Use text-lbta-red; error callout use bg-lbta-red/5 border-lbta-red/20; verify contrast for WCAG AAA with WebAIM if on light background

patterns.name: deploy-means-git-push-and-vercel-prod
patterns.when: User or compound workflow says 'deploy' or 'review then validate then deploy'
patterns.example: 1) Commit changes. 2) git push to GitHub (origin). 3) Run vercel --prod (or ensure Vercel Git integration deploys from main). Do not stop at preview deploy or local build only.

patterns.name: hero-bottom-content-no-clip
patterns.when: Hero section has bottom-pinned content (absolute bottom-0) and overflow-hidden
patterns.example: Use min-h-screen so section is always full viewport height; otherwise on short viewports the content block extends above the section and gets clipped (e.g. first line of headline 'Tennis, as it should be taught' cut off).

patterns.name: hero-cross-browser-headline-clearance
patterns.when: Hero with fixed header and bottom-pinned headline; headline must be fully visible on load in all major browsers
patterns.example: Content area: top-24 md:top-28 (not just header height); pt-16 md:pt-20 on content block. Avoids Chrome/Safari/Firefox and zoom differences clipping the top of the headline.

patterns.name: plan-implementation-status-before-next
patterns.when: Starting work from existing plans (e.g. schedules UX, brand guide, Canva)
patterns.example: Review code against plan checklist; update plans/plan-implementation-status-*.md. Avoids re-implementing work already done.

patterns.name: scroll-spy-active-section-tall-sections
patterns.when: Sticky in-page nav with IntersectionObserver to highlight current section; page sections are tall and rootMargin shrinks the effective viewport band
patterns.example: Pick the section with the largest intersection ratio (e.g. ratio > 0), not a fixed threshold like 0.2. With rootMargin '-20% 0 -60% 0', ratios stay small; 'best ratio' still identifies the section most in view.

patterns.name: thank-you-type-whitelist
patterns.when: Single confirmation page supports multiple types via query param (e.g. /thank-you?type=trial|program|year|scholarship)
patterns.example: getThankYouType(raw): const type = Array.isArray(raw) ? raw[0] : raw; whitelist only allowed values; default to 'trial'. Use type only to key a COPY_BY_TYPE map (no user input in DOM or redirect URL).

patterns.name: modal-success-focus-primary
patterns.when: Modal shows a success state that replaces the form (e.g. registration received)
patterns.example: useRef on primary CTA (e.g. 'View next steps'); useEffect(() => { if (isSuccess && isOpen) successPrimaryRef.current?.focus(); }, [isSuccess, isOpen]). Ensures keyboard/screen-reader users get focus in the new content.

patterns.name: footer-area-deep-water-contrast
patterns.when: Any component rendered inside the footer (same bg-brand-deep-water as main footer)
patterns.example: Use text-white/70 or higher for body and description text (e.g. NewsletterForm description). Matches footer tagline and links; avoids text-white/50 which can fail 7:1 on deep-water.

patterns.name: plan-doc-checkbox-hygiene
patterns.when: Updating implementation plans after work is done
patterns.example: Tick [x] only for steps/criteria that are actually implemented; leave [ ] with a note (e.g. 'verify when ready') for items like Lighthouse that need a later check. Keeps plans as single source of truth for status.

patterns.name: external-system-setup-checklist
patterns.when: Integrating with an external system (e.g. ActiveCampaign) where code sends data but the user configures automations in the UI
patterns.example: Create one actionable doc (e.g. docs/activecampaign-setup-checklist.md) with step-by-step 'In AC, do the following' and IDs from code (list id, tag ids). Cross-reference from the main ops doc so the user has one place to set up confirmations.

patterns.name: optional-dual-write-after-primary
patterns.when: Adding an optional second integration (e.g. GHL SMS) that runs after the primary integration (e.g. ActiveCampaign)
patterns.example: Env-gate (hasEnvVar for all required vars); after primary success call void sendToGHL(payload); same payload as primary (already validated); on failure log only, never throw or change API response

patterns.name: external-api-error-log-truncate
patterns.when: Logging errors from third-party API calls (create contact, add to workflow, etc.)
patterns.example: const text = await res.text(); const safeMsg = text.length > 100 ? text.slice(0, 100) + '…' : text; console.error('[GHL] Failed:', res.status, safeMsg); avoids PII or internal data in logs

patterns.name: env-checker-no-values
patterns.when: Providing a script or CLI to verify env vars for an integration (AC, GHL, etc.)
patterns.example: Script reads only process.env[key]; prints 'set' or 'missing' per key; never logs values. Exit 0 when required vars set, else 1. npm run check:env for local/CI.

patterns.name: ops-cli-entry-points
patterns.when: A key ops flow (leads, deploy platform) has a check or status command
patterns.example: Expose as named npm script: lead → scripts/lead.js (env + where leads go + doc link); check:vercel → npx vercel whoami && npx vercel list --yes. Never print secrets; point to docs when relevant.

patterns.name: vercel-env-add-via-api
patterns.when: Adding sensitive env vars to Vercel programmatically (e.g. AC, GHL keys)
patterns.example: Script reads VERCEL_TOKEN from gitignored .env.vercel; secret values from process.env at runtime; POST to Vercel API (e.g. /v10/projects/{id}/env); never commit or log secret values. scripts/vercel-env-add.js.

patterns.name: lead-pipeline-smoke-test
patterns.when: Lead pipeline (AC + optional GHL) is configured and you want to verify without creating test contacts
patterns.example: Provide npm run smoke:lead that runs env check + optional read-only AC/GHL ping (connection-check --ping); optional SMOKE_BASE_URL to verify live site returns 200. scripts/smoke-lead.js.

patterns.name: connection-check-same-base-as-app
patterns.when: Script pings the same external API the app uses (e.g. GHL workflows)
patterns.example: Use same base URL (GHL_API_BASE or default Lead Connector) and same auth/version (Version: 2021-07-28 when leadconnectorhq.com) so pass/fail matches runtime behavior; avoid legacy-only URL in script while app uses v2.

patterns.name: form-handler-never-500-on-valid-payload
patterns.when: Form API (newsletter, book, register) where validation can pass but optional integrations (AC, GHL, storeLead) or rate limit might fail
patterns.example: Wrap rateLimit in try/catch, allow on failure; parseJsonBody returns 400 on any parse error (no throw); after validation pass: void sendToGHL(...).catch(log); void storeLead(...).catch(log); return 200. User always gets success when payload is valid.

patterns.name: overview-and-camp-prices-in-data
patterns.when: Overview cards need 'from' price fallbacks or camp week pricing has special cases (e.g. 3-day week)
patterns.example: programsOverviewFallbacks in pricing-supplemental.json; year2026 camp entries with threeDayFullPrice, threeDayHalfPrice; lib/programs-data and lib/camps-data read from data and use code fallback only as last resort (?? 25 etc.).

patterns.name: next-image-sizes-for-logos
patterns.when: Using next/image for logos or fixed-size images (Header, Footer, PartnershipSection)
patterns.example: Add sizes attribute: Header logo sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100px, 120px"; Footer logo sizes="180px"; partner logos sizes="144px". Meets best-practice and validation.

patterns.name: rate-limit-try-catch-all-form-routes
patterns.when: Any API route (book, newsletter, register, etc.) uses rateLimit() from lib/rate-limit
patterns.example: let rateLimitResult; try { rateLimitResult = await rateLimit(key, RATE_LIMITS.xxx); } catch { rateLimitResult = { allowed: true, remaining: RATE_LIMITS.xxx.maxRequests, resetTime: Date.now() + RATE_LIMITS.xxx.interval }; }. Prevents 500 when KV is down or unavailable.

patterns.name: footer-all-text-70-deep-water
patterns.when: Any text or icon color on bg-brand-deep-water (footer, NewsletterForm, etc.)
patterns.example: Use text-white/70 or text-white/80 for tagline, links, labels, social icons, and placeholders. No text-white/60 or placeholder-white/30; strict 'all text on deep-water ≥ 70%' for validation 100/100.

patterns.name: section-overline-explicit-brand-color
patterns.when: Section has an overline or eyebrow label (e.g. 'Our Network') in a brand-only section
patterns.example: Add text-brand-pacific-dusk (or appropriate token) to the overline element so the section uses only brand tokens; avoids inherited/gray and satisfies design-system validation.

patterns.name: schedule-data-single-entry
patterns.when: Schedules page or any component needs seasonal program lists (winter, spring, summer, fall)
patterns.example: Use getWinter2026Programs(), getSpringProgramsForDisplay(), getSummerProgramsForDisplay(), getFall2026Programs() from @/lib/programs-data; no component imports data/winter2026.json or other season JSON directly.

patterns.name: program-price-display-monthly-primary
patterns.when: Component shows a single 'from' price and program has both monthly and session (1x/2x/3x) pricing
patterns.example: If monthly != null and (no session prices OR monthly <= min(sessionPrices)), display monthly first (e.g. $120/mo); otherwise show min session price with From when multiple tiers. ProgramRow getPrice().

patterns.name: program-data-shape-consistent-seasons
patterns.when: Adding or editing program entries in season JSON (winter2026, spring-summer-2026, fall2026)
patterns.example: Use same optional keys (matchPlay, pricingNote) where applicable; same program display name and ages (e.g. Adult Beginner 1 — True Beginner, NTRP 1.0-2.0) across seasons.

patterns.name: webhook-rate-limit
patterns.when: External webhook route (e.g. ActiveCampaign POST)
patterns.example: At start of POST: rateLimit('webhook:'+ip, RATE_LIMITS.webhook) with try/catch; allow on failure. Production: return 401 when secret env unset; log only non-PII in prod.

patterns.name: single-component-re-export
patterns.when: Consolidating duplicate component (e.g. root vs ui/)
patterns.example: Keep canonical in components/ui/AnimatedSection.tsx; components/AnimatedSection.tsx: export { default } from '@/components/ui/AnimatedSection' so @/components/AnimatedSection and @/components/ui/AnimatedSection both resolve to same implementation.

patterns.name: parallel-add-tags
patterns.when: Applying multiple ActiveCampaign tags to one contact
patterns.example: Promise.all(tagIds.map(id => addTag(contactId, id))); aggregate success/failed from results. Faster than sequential loop.

patterns.name: modal-pricing-from-data
patterns.when: Registration or program modal displays pricing (e.g. RegistrationModal, form-config prePopulateData)
patterns.example: Load pricing from same /data sources (programs-data, camps-data, year2026) or a single shared config; avoid duplicate strings in lib/form-config.ts so single source of truth is respected.

patterns.name: single-type-from-zod
patterns.when: Data passed server→client with runtime validation (e.g. schedules year2026, leagues)
patterns.example: Export type from lib (e.g. LeaguesData = LeaguesDataValidated, Year2026Sections = Year2026SectionsValidated in schedule-schemas.ts); parse on server; client imports type; no 'as unknown as T' at boundary.

patterns.name: schedule-slot-venue-consistent
patterns.when: Adding or editing schedule slot note or location text in season JSON
patterns.example: Use same venue wording as rest of file (e.g. 'LB High School' not 'LBHS'); Pattern Recognizer flags abbreviation vs full name inconsistency

patterns.name: form-config-location-matches-data
patterns.when: Program has an entry in lib/form-config.ts (prePopulateData) and in data/*.json
patterns.example: When updating program location in winter2026.json (e.g. Youth Development at LBHS), update form-config prePopulateData.location for that programId so forms and schedules show same location

patterns.name: program-description-lean
patterns.when: Writing or editing program description in season JSON when location and schedule slot notes already carry venue info
patterns.example: Description: 'Competitive development for green ball players. Invitation-based. Same pricing as Youth Dev tiers.' — omit repeating Moulton Meadows / Alta Laguna / Friday LBHS; let location and slot.note carry where/when

patterns.name: placement-inquiry-unified
patterns.when: Program is level-based, invitation-based, or coach-approved (e.g. Youth Development, UTR Green Dot Competitive, High Performance)
patterns.example: Use (1) description stating level/age and that exceptions may qualify with coach approval; (2) optional pricingNote for edge case (e.g. under age, different level); (3) optional inquiryLabel and link to /contact?program=...&inquiry=placement. Apply consistently so 'with coaches approval they can join' is unified across programs.

patterns.name: login-redirect-param-validate
patterns.when: Login page uses ?next= for redirect after auth
patterns.example: Validate to same-origin path only: safeRedirectTarget(next) — allow only relative paths under app prefix (e.g. /coach-hub), reject //, full URLs, and non-path input. Prevents open redirect (OWASP A01).

patterns.name: password-max-length-schema
patterns.when: Auth schema for password (e.g. coach hub, any single shared secret)
patterns.example: z.string().min(1, 'Password is required').max(512, 'Invalid password'); generic message avoids length leakage; caps DoS from huge buffer/timingSafeEqual.

patterns.name: modal-overlay-focus-trap-escape
patterns.when: Implementing dialogs or overlays (BinderOverlay, GuideOverlay, any modal)
patterns.example: Add onKeyDown(e => e.key === 'Escape' && onClose()); focus first focusable on open (useEffect or ref); optionally aria-describedby. Ensures keyboard users can close and focus does not leave.

patterns.name: tab-list-aria
patterns.when: Tab strip (buttons that switch panes)
patterns.example: Container role="tablist"; each tab role="tab" aria-selected={activeTab === id}; pane role="tabpanel" id and aria-labelledby pointing to tab. Screen readers then announce tabs correctly.

patterns.name: range-input-label
patterns.when: Range input (e.g. players on court slider)
patterns.example: Give input id="players-on-court"; label htmlFor="players-on-court"; optionally aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax for current value.

patterns.name: mobile-audit-baseline
patterns.when: Implementing or auditing mobile experience (plan Phase 1 or before shipping mobile changes)
patterns.example: Audit at 320px, 375px, 768px: no horizontal scroll; primary actions min-h-[48px] min-w-[48px]; form inputs 16px+ (text-base or text-[16px]) to avoid iOS zoom; scroll-mt on anchor sections; modals max-h-[90vh] with internal overflow-y-auto. See plans/mobile-audit-phase1-checklist.md.

patterns.name: auth-contract-verification-script
patterns.when: Protected route has an auth API (e.g. password login) that returns 400 for invalid body and 401 for wrong secret
patterns.example: Add scripts/verify-*-auth.mjs that fetch POST {} → expect 400, POST { password: 'wrong' } → expect 401. Run with dev server: COACH_HUB_SECRET=secret ./node_modules/.bin/next dev; then node scripts/verify-coach-hub-auth.mjs. Document in validation summary or README so validation can reach 100/100 without manual curl.

patterns.name: modal-focus-trap-tab-wrap
patterns.when: Modal or overlay already has initial focus and Escape-to-close; WCAG expects focus to stay inside dialog
patterns.example: In onKeyDown: if Tab and focus on last focusable, preventDefault and focus first; if Shift+Tab and focus on first, preventDefault and focus last. Query focusables from dialog ref (button, [href], input, select, textarea, [tabindex]:not([tabindex="-1"])); filter disabled.

patterns.name: single-expand-cards-in-list
patterns.when: Page shows multiple expandable cards (e.g. ProgramCard) and expanded state shows a fixed/sticky bar
patterns.example: Parent state: expandedProgramId: string | null. Each card gets isExpanded={expandedProgramId === program.id}, onToggle={() => setExpandedProgramId(prev => prev === program.id ? null : program.id)}. Only one card expanded at a time → one sticky bar.

patterns.name: success-timeout-ref-cleanup
patterns.when: Component (page or modal) uses setTimeout then setState (e.g. clear form after 5s success)
patterns.example: Store timeout id in useRef; useEffect cleanup: return () => { if (ref.current) clearTimeout(ref.current); }. Prevents setState after unmount.

patterns.name: next15-page-searchparams-async
patterns.when: Next 15+ App Router page needs searchParams (or params)
patterns.example: export default async function Page({ searchParams }: { searchParams?: Promise<{ type?: string | string[] }> }) { const params = await searchParams; const type = getType(params?.type); ... }

patterns.name: thank-you-all-links-focus-ring
patterns.when: Thank-you or confirmation page with tel, mailto, Return home, or app download links
patterns.example: Every <a> and <Link> gets focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-victoria-cove focus:ring-offset-2 rounded-sm; on dark sections use focus:ring-white focus:ring-offset-black. Step/card accents use brand tokens (brand-victoria-cove), not raw blue.

patterns.name: form-success-response-ok-and-data-success
patterns.when: Client submits to API and treats response as success
patterns.example: const raw = await response.json().catch(() => ({})); const data = typeof raw === 'object' && raw !== null && 'success' in raw ? raw as { success?: boolean; error?: string } : { success: false }; if (response.ok && data.success) { ... } else { setError(data.error ?? fallback); }

patterns.name: modal-timeout-ref-cleanup
patterns.when: Modal uses setTimeout/setInterval (e.g. success redirect or auto-close)
patterns.example: Store id in useRef; clear in handleClose and in useEffect cleanup (return () => { if (ref.current) { clearTimeout(ref.current); ref.current = null; } }); so timer is always cleared on unmount or close.

patterns.name: league-drop-in-in-data-and-ui
patterns.when: League/division has both season price and per-session drop-in
patterns.example: Add optional drop_in (number) to division in data; extend schema (e.g. utrDivisionSchema) with drop_in: z.number().optional(); row component accepts optional dropIn prop and renders e.g. 'Drop-in $X' under main price (desktop and mobile). Single source in data.

patterns.name: contextual-private-booking-coach-slug
patterns.when: User can start private lesson request from coach-specific context (bio, schedules table)
patterns.example: Use URL /book?type=private&coach=<slug>; book page reads coach param, resolves slug→display name (e.g. lib/coach-slug from data/coaches.json), passes defaultCoach to private modal; modal accepts optional defaultCoach and on open sets form coach to that value.

patterns.name: coach-headshot-cache-bust
patterns.when: Coach headshots live at static paths and are replaced; CDN/browser may serve old image
patterns.example: Export COACH_IMAGE_VERSION and coachImageSrc(path) in lib/coaches-data.ts; use coachImageSrc() for all coach image src (cards, founder, bio pages, OG); bump COACH_IMAGE_VERSION when replacing assets.

patterns.name: section-horizon-key-headings
patterns.when: Brand Guide: key section headings (hero, section eyebrows) need horizon accent
patterns.example: Add <div className="section-horizon ... opacity-90" aria-hidden="true" /> under headline or eyebrow (e.g. CoachesHero, FounderSection, CoachingTeamSection); do not add extra border classes.

patterns.name: next-image-local-patterns-complete
patterns.when: Next.js 15+ with images.localPatterns (explicit allowlist for local src)
patterns.example: In next.config.js include every prefix used by <Image src="/...">: { pathname: '/images/**' }, { pathname: '/logos/**' }, { pathname: '/photos/**' }. After adding paths, smoke: curl -sI "https://site/_next/image?url=%2Flogos%2F...&w=256&q=75" → 200 not 400.

patterns.name: fixed-header-opaque-on-light-text
patterns.when: Fixed site header with dark body text (e.g. brand-pacific-dusk) sits over full-bleed photo/video hero
patterns.example: Use solid bg-brand-morning-light (no /90 opacity) plus border-b border-brand-pacific-dusk/[0.08]; avoid backdrop-blur + translucent fill that lets dark pixels blend under nav links (WCAG contrast).

patterns.name: layout-primitives-sections
patterns.when: Adding new vibe sections (masonry, zigzag, bento, asymmetric)
patterns.example: Use components/sections: MasonryGrid for community/galleries (varied spans), ZigzagSection for alternating image/text, SplitSection for 50/50. Reference /legacy-working-assets/; ensure path in next.config images.localPatterns.

patterns.name: plan-aware-review
patterns.when: Running compound review after plan-driven implementation
patterns.example: Pass to each review agent: plan summary (objective, out of scope, acceptance checklist, files to create/modify) plus code/diff. Enables scope compliance and acceptance-driven validation.

patterns.name: chat-suggested-actions
patterns.when: Chat widget or assistant UI needs to help users discover capabilities
patterns.example: After welcome message, render navigation-only action buttons (Book a Trial, View Programs, Contact Us) that link to pages. Use brand tokens, min-h-[48px], focus-visible:ring. Only show actions the backend can fulfill or that navigate to pages.

patterns.name: api-contracts-documentation
patterns.when: Building agent tools or maintaining API consistency between frontend and agents
patterns.example: Create docs/api-contracts.md with request shapes (TypeScript types), response formats, validation notes, and links to lib/validations.ts schemas. Document all user-facing endpoints (book, register-program, register-year, newsletter, scholarship, chat, coach-hub). Keep in sync when adding/changing endpoints.

patterns.name: help-page-with-quick-links
patterns.when: Users need to discover site capabilities or get help
patterns.example: Create /help page with: quick links (Programs & Pricing, Book a Trial, Meet Our Coaches, Contact Us), FAQ link, contact info (phone, email, address). Link from chat footer as 'What can you do?'. Server Component, brand tokens, focus rings on all links.

patterns.name: activecampaign-legacy-api-fallback
patterns.when: ActiveCampaign v3 API returns 405 or fails for campaign operations (e.g. send test)
patterns.example: Use legacy admin API: GET {base}/admin/api.php?api_action=campaign_send&campaignid=...&messageid=...&action=test&email=...&type=mime. Resolve messageid from GET /api/3/campaigns/{id}/campaignMessages. See send_test.py and docs/ACTIVECAMPAIGN_EMAIL_DESIGNER_LIMITATION.md.

patterns.name: data-driven-carousel-slides
patterns.when: Building an auto-rotating hero carousel (e.g. Player Success) with multiple slides
patterns.example: Define slides as typed array in data JSON (e.g. homepage-copy.json results.slides[]) with image, imageAlt, objectPosition, subline, ctaLabel, ctaHref. Component receives typed props (PlayerSuccessSlide[]), uses useEffect interval with pause-on-hover, useReducedMotion for a11y, aria-roledescription=carousel, and dot navigation with aria-current. See PlayerSuccessCarousel.tsx.

patterns.name: object-position-top-weighted-hero
patterns.when: Full-bleed hero image with object-cover that crops heads/faces
patterns.example: Use objectPosition: '50% 10%' to '50% 15%' (top-weighted) for player photos so faces stay in frame. Store per-slide in data JSON so each image can have its own crop. Default to '50% 12%' when not specified. Pair with taller min-h (e.g. min-h-[65vh] lg:min-h-[76vh]) to reduce vertical crop pressure.

patterns.name: facility-image-intent-mapping
patterns.when: Assigning coastal/facility photos to specific page sections
patterns.example: Map by visual intent: hero-ocean-view-tennis-courts-sunset.webp for full-bleed hero/OG; overview-coastal-tennis-facility-ocean.webp for destination/scale; detail-sunlit-blue-tennis-courts.webp for detail/schedules strip; coaching-private-tennis-lesson-drill.webp for private lesson narrative. Store in data JSON and document in docs/photo-map-routing.md.

patterns.name: player-photo-canonical-path
patterns.when: Referencing player/success-story photos across multiple surfaces
patterns.example: Store canonical player photos under public/images/success-stories/{slug}.webp (current) or public/images/players/{slug}/ (planned multi-photo). Reference the same canonical path from homepage carousel data, success-stories page, and OG metadata. Avoid duplicating files across directories.

patterns.name: program-landing-single-source-from-json
patterns.when: Program page (e.g. UTR Match Play) needs season copy, divisions, modal payload, form prefill
patterns.example: Create lib facade (e.g. utr-match-play.ts) over leagues-2026.json; export getUtrSeasonLabel, getUtrDivisionsForPage, getUtrCircuitModalData, getUtrCircuitFormDuration; page, LuxuryYearModal data, form-config all import. Single source of truth.

patterns.name: form-prefill-duration-from-data
patterns.when: form-config prePopulateData.duration for program with season dates in JSON
patterns.example: Derive via helper (e.g. getUtrCircuitFormDuration()) that reads seasonLabel from leagues JSON; add test asserting duration contains year and matches JSON. Avoids date drift.

patterns.name: player-success-hero-contain-no-crop
patterns.when: Player Success carousel (or full-bleed people hero) must show full subject with no cropping of heads/faces
patterns.example: In homepage-copy.json results.slides[], set imageFit: "contain" per slide; PlayerSuccessCarousel uses object-contain and bg-brand-deep-water letterboxing. Pair with well-framed source art. Tradeoff: less edge-to-edge than cover.

patterns.name: cloudinary-next-image-delivery
patterns.when: Using Cloudinary URLs as next/image src
patterns.example: https://res.cloudinary.com/{cloud}/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/v{version}/{public_id}.{ext}; ensure next.config images.remotePatterns includes hostname res.cloudinary.com. Prefetch in carousel uses same href string.

patterns.name: hero-pricing-hint-line
patterns.when: Homepage hero should reduce 'hidden pricing' friction without duplicating rates
patterns.example: homepage-copy.json: pricingHint + pricingHintHref → /schedules; HomeHero renders text link under subline (Victoria Cove underline). Extend events.heroCta with variant pricing_hint for GA4.

patterns.name: unavailable-offering-landing
patterns.when: Legacy URL must stay live but the product is paused
patterns.example: Honest H1/copy; metadata/OG aligned; hero image from main program art (e.g. utr-match-play-hero.webp) not orphan legacy path; primary CTA to substitute product + secondary to /schedules

patterns.name: utr-players-batch-rpc-atomic
patterns.when: Saving roster player batches with mixed new/existing records in UTR admin
patterns.example: Use one RPC (upsert_players_batch) for full payload, with DB-side UUID generation for missing ids and ON CONFLICT(id) updates. Avoid split insert+upsert client logic.

patterns.name: utr-roster-normalized-name-guard
patterns.when: Accepting roster writes where downstream systems resolve players by normalized display name
patterns.example: Normalize name via trim+lowercase+whitespace collapse; reject duplicates in payload and conflicts with existing roster using 409 before write.

patterns.name: color-ball-badge-dedupe-dual-layer
patterns.when: Saving Color Ball attendance + coach badges per week
patterns.example: Dedupe badge tokens in API request transformation and enforce DISTINCT in SQL function insert to keep duplicate badge tokens idempotent.

patterns.name: supabase-repair-then-push-latest-cli
patterns.when: Supabase push reports remote migration version mismatch or drift
patterns.example: Use npx supabase@latest; run targeted migration repair for the missing version, then db push. Avoid repeated push loops before reconciliation.

patterns.name: per-page-self-referencing-canonical
patterns.when: Adding canonical tags to a Next.js App Router site to consolidate duplicate-content signal
patterns.example: export const metadata: Metadata = { alternates: { canonical: '/programs/junior' }, title: '...' }. Root layout sets ONLY metadataBase. 33 public routes each declare their own canonical; landing variants (e.g. /high-performance-pathway) canonical to their hub (/programs/high-performance).

patterns.name: post-deploy-live-smoke-test
patterns.when: After any production deploy — especially SEO / config bundles where static validation passes but route-handler precedence or edge caching could hide bugs
patterns.example: curl the live domain for key markers: robots.txt content, canonical tags on 3-6 sample routes, noindex on protected routes, HTTP 200 on sitemap routes. Caught the app/robots.txt shadow bug that prerendered-HTML validation missed.

patterns.name: atomic-commits-per-logical-concern
patterns.when: Bundling multiple independent changes into one deploy
patterns.example: 8 atomic commits for one PR: phone fix, robots.txt, canonical-root-layout, noindex, hp-canonical, hero-test, remove-root-canonical, per-page-canonicals, robots-shadow-hotfix. Each reversible via git revert <sha> without touching the others.

patterns.name: build-output-validation-fallback
patterns.when: Live HTTP smoke testing blocked by port conflicts, stale dev servers, or network — need to validate metadata before pushing
patterns.example: grep .next/server/app/*.html for canonical tags, robots meta, key copy. Works for prerendered static routes; flag dynamic routes (force-dynamic, searchParams) for a live check later. Faster than booting a fresh dev server and doesn't mask caching issues.

patterns.name: shadow-file-detection-script
patterns.when: About to edit a static asset (robots.txt, sitemap.xml, manifest.json, etc.) in a Next.js App Router project
patterns.example: ls app/<name>* public/<name>* — if both exist, app/ wins. Pick one: delete the other or edit the winner.

patterns.name: segment-only-titles-with-template
patterns.when: Root layout declares metadata.title.template (e.g. '%s | Brand'); child pages need their own titles
patterns.example: Child page: `title: 'Tennis Training FAQ'` (segment only, no brand suffix). Root template appends '| Laguna Beach Tennis Academy'. Final: 'Tennis Training FAQ | Laguna Beach Tennis Academy' (49 chars). For pages where the brand should not be appended (e.g. the homepage), use `title: { absolute: 'Full Custom Title' }` to bypass the template.

patterns.name: rendered-output-length-validation
patterns.when: Validating any char-length constraint (titles ≤60, descriptions 150–160) on Next.js App Router pages
patterns.example: After build, `grep -oE '<title>[^<]+</title>' .next/server/app/<page>.html` and measure the content. Or curl the live URL and do the same. Never trust raw source length when metadata templates, frameworks, or any other layer may transform the output.

patterns.name: privacy-first-content-pattern
patterns.when: Founder/stakeholder directive: do not introduce family, student, or client names in new work
patterns.example: Replace named testimonials with a 'Read reviews on Google' link that reads the live review count from a single JSON source of truth. Replace name-bearing schema Reviews with AggregateRating only. Replace name-bearing founder bios with credentials + geography. Scope guard: explicitly distinguish pre-existing named content (out of scope) from new work (in scope); track pre-existing cleanup as a separate dedicated PR.

patterns.name: aggregate-rating-invisible-only
patterns.when: Brand aesthetic rejects visible rating widgets but SERP stars are still valuable
patterns.example: Keep `aggregateRating` block in JSON-LD schema (components/SEOSchemas.tsx or equivalent) so Google renders ★ in search results. Remove all on-page copy that mentions the rating number or review count. Hero trust stats use heritage-flavored facts ('2020 Founded', 'Official City Partner') instead of a Google Rating stat. Net: full CTR lift, zero brand cost.

patterns.name: brand-split-visible-vs-invisible-seo
patterns.when: A standard SEO technique (ratings, social proof widgets, aggregator links) conflicts with a founder-directed aesthetic (Aman / boutique / editorial)
patterns.example: Split into two layers. Visible (what users read): governed by brand aesthetic — editorial, credentials, heritage, restraint. Invisible (what Google parses): JSON-LD schema, meta tags, OpenGraph — every SEO technique stays. Users get Aman. Google gets the full SEO surface. This is how luxury brands rank for their city without a Tripadvisor widget on the page.

patterns.name: build-codegen-as-canonical-source
patterns.when: Running ship:gate / quality-gate / build and tracked files become dirty — codegen step (tokens:build, data:sync, etc.) auto-rewrote them with canonical values
patterns.example: When ship:gate reports 'tracked files have uncommitted changes' AFTER a successful build + lint + tests, DO NOT revert the changes. Diff them first. The codegen knew the canonical answer (e.g. 'Aug 29' camp end date) and resolved drift across JSON + components. Commit the codegen output + fix any remaining hardcoded literals in views that bypassed the data layer. This session: codegen auto-aligned year2026.json + spring-summer-2026.json + ProgramsTabView.tsx to Aug 29; I added matching fix to app/camps/page.tsx. Ops-audit blocker resolved without founder input.

patterns.name: reversible-pause-for-hidden-entity
patterns.when: An entity (coach, program, location, camp week) is temporarily paused — may return later. Need to hide from public surfaces but preserve the data for zero-cost reactivation
patterns.example: 1) Add optional `hidden?: boolean` + `hiddenReason?: string` to the entity type. 2) Set hidden=true in JSON data. 3) Add private getVisibleX() helper; all public getters + schema builders filter via it. 4) Single-link getters (getBySlug) stay UNFILTERED so direct URLs still resolve for reactivation testing. 5) Explicit robots: { index: false, follow: false } on the entity's detail page with a JSDoc comment describing the reactivation steps. 6) If the hidden entity was part of a fixed-layout grid (2x2), swap to grid-cols-N for the remaining N and note the revert path in a comment. This session: paused Michelle coach — 4 files touched, full reversibility.

patterns.name: ship-gate-commit-first
patterns.when: Running npm run ship:gate after making local changes
patterns.example: Always commit tracked changes BEFORE running ship:gate. The clean-tree check is the final gate — it fires hard even if build/lint/tests all passed. Pattern: `git add ... && git commit -m '...' && npm run ship:gate`. If codegen auto-modifies files DURING ship:gate (e.g. tokens:build rewriting JSON), that's expected; commit those too, then re-run. NEVER try to 'run ship:gate first then commit' — you'll spend time debugging a guardrail, not a real problem.

patterns.name: founder-decision-via-structured-question
patterns.when: A blocking decision has 2-4 distinct options with different engineering paths (e.g. active/departed/paused/status-quo for coach status)
patterns.example: Use the AskQuestion tool with explicit labeled options that each describe the engineering consequence. Saves a round trip vs. vague text reply. Works because founder only has to read + select, not formulate + type. This session: Michelle pause decision took 30 seconds of founder time, zero clarification round trips.

patterns.name: founder-voice-memo-decode
patterns.when: Founder sends voice-to-text feedback with garbled phrases (e.g. 'among standards' for 'Aman standards', 'I went off the goal is deep and throws you want' as garble)
patterns.example: Parse: ignore mistranscribed filler, anchor on the clear nouns + .cursorrules brand references. 'Aman standards' + 'marketing and what they do' → compare current UI against aman.com patterns (no ratings, no aggregator links, long-form editorial, heritage stats). Restate the decoded intent back before executing so founder can course-correct if I misread.

patterns.name: full-compound-loop-in-one-session
patterns.when: Scoped bundle (≤12 files, known shape, memory loaded) where Plan/Work/Review/Validate/Deploy/Learn can run sequentially in one session
patterns.example: SEO P1 bundle: 4 hours total. Plan with memory auto-load + audit-verification caught 1 wasted commit. Work in 8 atomic commits. Review happened inline (mid-work) when live smoke caught title-template bug — fixed in one follow-up commit before push. Validate via build output. Deploy to Vercel prod, smoke test immediately. Learn bakes the new patterns back. Entire loop is compounding, not linear.

patterns.name: bot-fact-source-of-truth-hierarchy
patterns.when: Building a bot/front-desk GPT knowledge base for an org with structured data files and a public website
patterns.example: LBTA bot facts lookup order: (1) data/year2026.json [calendar, base pricing, camps] → (2) data/{season}2026.json [per-season program rows] → (3) data/leagues-2026.json + private-rates.json + pricing-supplemental.json → (4) KB markdown derived from above → (5) UI mirror /schedules. When answer not in 1-3, bot says 'let me get the desk on this' and routes to support@ or (949) 534-0457. Every Q&A in gpt/03-KNOWLEDGE-BRAIN/23-bot-knowledge-base-2026.md ends with **Sources:** line + Escalate/Self-serve. Never invent numbers.

patterns.name: acceptance-script-as-validator
patterns.when: Shipping a docs/data file claiming numerical facts (≥10 prices, times, or thresholds)
patterns.example: Write a Python assertion script next to the doc: one assert per claim, comparing the doc's number to the source JSON. This loop ran 49 assertions in <100ms and caught LiveBall NTRP drift mechanically. Pattern: data_checks = [(label, get(json,'path')==expected), ...]; report passed/failed counts. Re-run after every correction. Cheap; obvious in hindsight; saves an hour of manual cross-checking per pass. The script becomes a living spec.

patterns.name: founder-correction-cross-surface-sweep
patterns.when: Founder corrects a single fact (price, level, schedule, policy) that may be mirrored in multiple files
patterns.example: After applying the correction in one place, grep the corrected term across data/**/*.json (incl. data/coach-hub/), components/**/*.{tsx,ts}, and gpt/03-KNOWLEDGE-BRAIN/**.md. Resolve every match in the SAME loop. Plan should list ALL surfaces upfront via an initial grep, not just the obvious ones. This loop's example: LiveBall NTRP correction touched 6 files: 3 season JSONs + coach-hub mirror + ProgramsTabView (hardcoded card) + brain doc 02. Almost shipped Saturday-Adult-Intermediate-not-separate correction without catching ProgramCard.tsx JSDoc + brain doc 02 + coach-hub mirror.

patterns.name: bot-kb-q-and-a-structure-2026
patterns.when: Authoring a bot/AI knowledge base for retrieval (not narrative reading)
patterns.example: Each Q&A: '### Q{n}. {canonical question}' heading + '**Tags:** k1, k2, k3' line + '**Answer:**' (≤120 words, one factual anchor) + '**Sources:**' (≥1 path/URL) + '**Escalate:** phone/email' or '**Self-serve:** URL'. Plus two compounding appendices: Appendix B (coverage matrix vs SMS/support log → Q-numbers, so we know what we DIDN'T cover) and Appendix C (reconciliation notes — Resolved YYYY-MM-DD vs Open). Founder confirmations move items from Open→Resolved with timestamp; doc grows without rotting.

patterns.name: season-aware-pricing-quote
patterns.when: Quoting any LBTA program price in bot/AI/marketing copy
patterns.example: Never quote a single program price without anchoring to a season — per-session value is similar but absolute totals differ because Winter=13wk, Spring=10wk, Summer=11wk, Fall=16wk. Junior 1×/wk: $546 / $420 / $462 / $672 across seasons. Bot rule: 'Always confirm season first when quoting prices. If unsure which season, ask.' Encoded in KB header rules of engagement and in Q10 of 23-bot-knowledge-base-2026.md.

patterns.name: lib-level-form-pipeline-dedup
patterns.when: Form pipeline writes to a primary storage system (e.g. Supabase leads) from many API routes; need to dedupe accidental rapid resubmissions across all forms
patterns.example: Add 120s server-side dedup window IN the shared lib (e.g. lib/leads-store.ts), not per route. Pattern: const DEDUP_WINDOW_MS = 120_000; lookup recent (email, source) within window; skip insert + log if found; fail-open on lookup error (logging) rather than dropping leads. Single fix protects every route that calls the lib. LBTA uses this for storeLead() called from book/register/register-program/register-year/newsletter/scholarship/chat

patterns.name: useref-synchronous-double-submit-guard
patterns.when: React form modal where rapid double-clicks bypass the disabled prop because useState batches async (production shows 14 duplicate submissions in real LBTA data)
patterns.example: const submittingRef = useRef(false); in handleSubmit: e.preventDefault(); if (submittingRef.current) return; submittingRef.current = true; setIsSubmitting(true); /* ... */ finally { setIsSubmitting(false); submittingRef.current = false; }. Belt-and-suspenders with server-side dedup. Comment: 'Synchronous double-submit guard: useState updates batch async, so a fast double-click can fire two submissions before the disabled prop renders.'

patterns.name: multi-storage-lead-audit
patterns.when: Founder asks 'are leads being saved' or 'how many leads do we have'
patterns.example: Enumerate every storage target before answering. LBTA writes to: (1) Supabase leads table via storeLead(), (2) Notion 'Registrations' database via writeNotionLead(), (3) ActiveCampaign contacts via createOrUpdateContact, (4) GoHighLevel via sendToGHL, plus (5) Meta lead-ad forms write directly to Notion via Zapier (bypasses website code). Query each, dedupe by email + normalized phone, present the union. Flag persistent gaps as candidates for a unified-mirror cron

patterns.name: vercel-deploy-verify-via-timing
patterns.when: Need to confirm Vercel production deployed the latest commit but no VERCEL_TOKEN is available for API queries
patterns.example: Capture local HEAD SHA + commit time. After git push, run npx vercel ls --prod and check: (1) deployment 'created' time is within seconds of commit time (auto-trigger from git push), (2) status is ✓ Ready, (3) duration is plausible (40-90s for typical builds), (4) aliases include the production domain. Then HEAD curl the live domain for HTTP 200. The triple match (timing + ready + alias + 200) is sufficient verification when the API token isn't loaded

patterns.name: lead-export-csv-with-channel-classification
patterns.when: Founder needs an offline working copy of leads with 'class interested in' across multi-storage systems
patterns.example: One CSV with columns: Date, Time, Name, Email, Phone, Channel, Class / Program Interest, Details, Notion Page. Channel column distinguishes 'Trial Booking (Website)', 'Private Lesson Booking (Website)', 'Meta Lead Ad', 'Newsletter (Website)', 'Imported (filename)'. Use payload.program for website leads; payload.bookingType=='private' → 'Private Lesson'. Sort by created_at desc. Write to ~/Desktop/lbta-leads-ALL-{date}.csv. Single Python block reads from Supabase REST + Notion API and merges deduped by email + last-10-digit phone

patterns.name: notion-supabase-mirror-cron
patterns.when: Multiple lead/contact channels write to Notion (Meta lead-ad forms, Zapier integrations) but the codebase only writes to Supabase — need to unify so reporting + dashboards see everything
patterns.example: Vercel cron daily → /api/cron/mirror-meta-leads (Authorization: Bearer ${CRON_SECRET}) → mirrorMetaLeadsToSupabase({sinceMs}). Filter Notion query by created_time + Meta-channel signal (campaign_name | ad_name | form_name | platform). Idempotency via unique partial index on notion_page_id (text column added to leads table). Default scan window > cron interval (36h for daily) to recover from a one-off skipped run. storeLead() updated to dedup by notionPageId when set, falling back to (email, source) time-window dedup

patterns.name: auth-gated-admin-route-under-existing-prefix
patterns.when: Need an internal admin view (e.g. /admin/leads) but already have an auth-gated prefix (e.g. /coach-hub) with cookie path scoped to that prefix
patterns.example: Put the new view UNDER the existing prefix (/coach-hub/leads, not /admin/leads) so proxy.ts already gates it via /coach-hub/:path*. Cookie path matches. No new auth surface. For the matching API export route (/api/coach-hub/leads/export), add defense-in-depth cookie verification at handler entry since proxy.ts has a passthrough for /api/coach-hub/* paths. Pattern: server-rendered table with searchParams-based filter (no client JS for filter); brand tokens; force-dynamic + robots noindex

patterns.name: csv-export-shared-lib-server-only
patterns.when: Two surfaces (UI table + CSV download route) need the same shape of data and same row formatting
patterns.example: lib/leads-query.ts exports getAllLeads, channelLabel(row), programLabel(row), leadMatchesChannel(row, channel), leadsToCsv(rows). Both /coach-hub/leads (page) and /api/coach-hub/leads/export (CSV route) import from this lib. Single source of truth for channel buckets, friendly labels, and CSV column shape. RFC-4180 csvField() helper for quoting

patterns.name: vercel-env-add-no-newline
patterns.when: Adding a secret env var to Vercel that will be used in HTTP headers (Authorization: Bearer X, X-Webhook-Secret, etc.)
patterns.example: Use printf '%s' "$SECRET" | npx vercel env add NAME production. echo "$SECRET" | ... appends a trailing newline that gets captured into the env var value, causing Vercel build to fail with 'CRON_SECRET environment variable contains leading or trailing whitespace, which is not allowed in HTTP header values'. Symptom: Vercel deploy fails at build step within ~1s

patterns.name: supabase-unique-partial-index-for-mirror-idempotency
patterns.when: Mirroring rows from an external system (Notion, Airtable, Stripe) into Supabase and need DB-level idempotency without disrupting other inserts that don't have the source ID
patterns.example: alter table leads add column notion_page_id text; create unique index ... on leads (notion_page_id) where notion_page_id is not null; — partial index lets website-form leads (no notion_page_id, value NULL) coexist with mirrored leads (notion_page_id set, unique). storeLead handles the unique-violation error code (PG '23505') as success-equivalent for the mirror path. Lookup-and-skip cheaper than catching error in normal path; fallback handles parallel runs

patterns.name: separate-domain-scanners-over-scanroots-extension
patterns.when: Adding a new file domain (emails, brochures, generated HTML) with fundamentally different rules than existing scanRoots
patterns.example: Don't append 'assets/emails' to scanRoots = ['app', 'components']. Build a separate scanner with its own root (emailScanRoot), exemptions (emailExemptFiles), heuristics (emailCustomerFacingMarker), and rule set. Different domains = different scanners. Share the Hit type, not the rule logic. Pattern fits brand checker, lint, type-check extensions.

patterns.name: compose-paths-from-single-root-constant
patterns.when: Multiple paths reference a shared root directory (exempt files, sub-roots, etc.)
patterns.example: Bad: const emailExemptFiles = new Set(["assets/emails/lbta-spring-2026.html"]). Good: const emailExemptFiles = new Set([`${emailScanRoot}/lbta-spring-2026.html`]) (template literal). If emailScanRoot is renamed (e.g. "assets/email-templates"), the exempt path follows automatically. No silent drift between the root and downstream consumers.

patterns.name: cli-library-dual-mode-isMainModule-guard
patterns.when: Same .ts file should work as both a CLI (npm run script) AND an importable library (e.g. for tests)
patterns.example: At end of file: const isMainModule = typeof process.argv[1] === "string" && process.argv[1].endsWith("check-brand-usage.ts"); if (isMainModule) { main().catch(...) }. Lets tests import scanEmailTemplate without main() auto-running. Add comment "if you rename this file, update this guard" — endsWith is rename-fragile.

patterns.name: precompile-regex-outside-per-line-loops
patterns.when: A scanner runs N regex patterns over M lines
patterns.example: Bad: lines.forEach(line => for (hex of set) { const pattern = new RegExp(...); line.match(pattern) }) — compiles N×M times. Good: const patterns = [...set].map(hex => new RegExp(...)); lines.forEach(line => for (pattern of patterns) line.match(pattern)) — compiles N times. Negligible at N=1, compounds as the rule set grows. Costs nothing at small N, scales correctly.

patterns.name: three-layer-exception-documentation
patterns.when: Code intentionally violates a project rule (e.g. allowed grays in lib/email.ts despite strict brand-color policy)
patterns.example: (1) JSON config: tokens/lbta-web-tokens.json lbtaUtility.allowed list with rationale per entry. (2) JSDoc at use site: lib/email.ts BRAND COLOR POLICY comment header explains the exception. (3) Back-link to framework: comment cites docs/brand-token-system.md → "Adding an exception (escape-hatch playbook)." Future maintainers can find the exception, the reason, and the framework that justifies it without spelunking.

patterns.name: plan-aware-reviewer-agents
patterns.when: Dispatching review/validate agents on a diff that came from an executed plan
patterns.example: Pass to every agent: plan path + decisions taken (with defaults documented) + acceptance checklist + out-of-scope list. Without it, agents flag deferred items as missing ("why no Litmus test?") and miss verifying the plan acceptance. With it, agents verify scope compliance + acceptance + check for new issues only. Caught the post-fix regression in Round 2 where simplicity reviewer noticed two of the new "behavior" tests were still config-pinning in disguise.

patterns.name: round-N-review-with-prior-findings
patterns.when: Fix-forwarding after Round-1 review and dispatching Round-2 reviewers
patterns.example: Pass each Round-2 agent THEIR OWN Round-1 findings in the prompt: "You previously flagged X, Y, Z — verify the fix for each." Each agent verifies fixes for their findings. Provides accountability + tracks resolution rate (e.g. "5/5 resolved" in structured output). Round 2 score uplift across all 6 agents averaged +11 points and converted 4-of-6 "fixes-needed" to 6-of-6 "ready."

patterns.name: fix-forward-vs-revert-decision-rule
patterns.when: Reviewers find issues but the work is functionally correct (acceptance met, tests pass, deploy clean)
patterns.example: Decision rule: if STRICT mode passes AND acceptance is met, fix-forward in single follow-up commit. If STRICT mode fails OR acceptance is broken, revert OR amend. Round 1 found 10 issues but acceptance was 7/7 + STRICT green + 15/15 tests + deployed cleanly → fix-forward. Single commit 0cf42a4 addressed all 10; preserved value of original work + addressed quality concerns. Avg score 78 → 89.

patterns.name: two-pass-audit-curve1-curve2
patterns.when: Cross-cutting audit (mobile UX, a11y, performance, brand) where measurable issues and taste issues both need surfacing
patterns.example: Pass 1 = hard scorecard (axe critical=0, Lighthouse mobile A11y, CLS thresholds — pass/fail). Pass 2 = luxury-flow gap report (qualitative founder-voice, restraint, micro-interactions — Andrew-decision). Both score independently so neither blocks the other. Curve-1 drives remediation tickets; Curve-2 drives andrew-decisions.md questions with deadlines.

patterns.name: token-scanner-codemod-three-step
patterns.when: Design-token migration, naming refactor, or convention rollout that touches >20 files
patterns.example: Commit 1: add the new semantic token (pacific-dusk-soft = #3D4658) — pure addition, no behavior change. Commit 2: add an info-only scanner category (forbiddenTextOpacityOnLight) that warns but doesn't fail strict mode. Commit 3+: codemod batches per route, each ≤40 files with surface-aware context heuristic. Final: promote scanner to ERROR severity in strict mode after all batches land. Avoids big-bang refactor diff-shock.

patterns.name: plan-aware-review-of-plan-doc
patterns.when: Plan that touches >5 files, makes architectural choices (token names, schema shapes, public API), or proposes a fix where the diagnosis isn't independently verified
patterns.example: Run the same persona reviewers (correctness, maintainability, testing, kieran-typescript, simplicity, project-standards) against the plan markdown — not the eventual diff. Score the plan; surface must-fix items before Work starts. This loop's outcome: 5 must-fix items caught (hex value failing AAA, CLS hypothesis 20% complete, codemod blast radius, victoria-cove policy, strict-mode env var) — two would have shipped silently because dev tools wouldn't catch them.

patterns.name: cookie-driven-ssr-banner
patterns.when: Persistent UI state depends on a cookie (banner dismissed, user role, theme preference, session flag) and currently mounts/dismounts/resizes after hydration causing CLS
patterns.example: Read the cookie in the Server Component (e.g. app/layout.tsx via next/headers cookies()), pass the resolved state to the banner so it renders with correct hydration state in initial HTML. No useEffect-driven re-render = no layout shift. Eliminated SeasonBanner's universal 0.097 CLS without client hydration latency.

patterns.name: suspense-isolate-useSearchParams
patterns.when: Route's layout chunk depends on useSearchParams (or any client-only hook that resolves async) and that chunk causes CLS by mounting after hydration
patterns.example: Extract the useSearchParams-dependent UI into a child component wrapped in <Suspense fallback={<StableShapePlaceholder />}>. Parent layout is stable while param-driven slice resolves. Killed /contact's unique 0.402 CLS; final production CLS = 0.000.

patterns.name: sequential-review-validate-deploy-gates
patterns.when: Every shipped feature touching >5 files
patterns.example: Each phase's structured output gates the next: Review (88) → unlocks Validate; Validate (92) on production build → unlocks Deploy; Deploy preview → AC confirmed on real Vercel-served URL → unlocks main merge. Catches dev-mode-vs-prod-build discrepancies before push.

patterns.name: acceptance-as-exit-condition
patterns.when: Plan with measurable performance, accessibility, or correctness goals
patterns.example: Every Success Criterion mapped to a measurable Acceptance check tied to a tool/command BEFORE Work starts. AC1 axe critical=0 across N routes; AC2 Lighthouse mobile A11y ≥ X; AC3 CLS ≤ Y on route Z. Validate phase mechanically re-runs each AC; PASS/FAIL is the unambiguous gate.

patterns.name: preview-deploy-then-manual-main-merge
patterns.when: Default for any PR that changes production behavior
patterns.example: Push branch → Vercel preview deploys → run AC checks against the real Vercel-served preview (real region, real build, real headers) → Andrew reviews → manual git merge --no-ff to main → push → production deploy. Keeps Andrew in the loop for production promotion without slowing the verify cycle. Bypass only for hotfixes Andrew explicitly authorizes.

patterns.name: ship-gate-disclosure-when-wip-orthogonal
patterns.when: Tracked tree dirty at ship time with WIP that's verifiably unrelated to the PR's scope (parallel workstream)
patterns.example: Disclose the dirty file list in deploy-summary.md with confirmation that none are touched by the PR diff (git diff --name-only main..branch). Then split the WIP onto its own local branch (e.g. coach-hub-wip-2026-05-13) BEFORE the main merge — preserves both lineages, ship-gate runs against actual PR scope. Better than blanket hard-stop when WIP is provably orthogonal AND in-flight.

patterns.name: untracked-orphan-deps-stash-restore-pattern
patterns.when: Local ship-gate (next build) fails on untracked files importing exports that were just relocated to another branch — but the untracked files are NOT in any git history (Vercel won't see them)
patterns.example: Verify with `git ls-files --error-unmatch <file>` and `git cat-file -e <branch>:<file>` that the failing files are untracked everywhere. Move them to /tmp/<stash>/<original/path/> with mkdir -p preserving directory structure. Re-run ship:gate (now passes). Push. Wait for Vercel build (it never sees /tmp). Confirm health:prod + smoke. Restore from stash with mv. Document the discovery in deploy-summary so future-you remembers Vercel only sees committed files.
